legislation.title: Ley 35/2006 (IRPF), Artículo 9 — Residencia habitual
legislation.summary: An individual is tax resident in Spain for a calendar year if they spend more than 183 days of that year in Spanish territory. Spanish residents are taxed on their worldwide income. (Simplified; the statute also covers a centre-of-economic-interests test.)

legislation.title: UK Statutory Residence Test (RDR3)
legislation.summary: Under the Statutory Residence Test, an individual present in the UK for 183 days or more in a tax year is automatically UK resident. The full test also includes automatic-overseas, sufficient-ties and split-year rules (simplified here to the 183-day automatic test).

legislation.title: Abgabenordnung (AO) §§ 8–9 — Wohnsitz / gewöhnlicher Aufenthalt
legislation.summary: Germany treats an individual as unlimitedly tax liable where they have a domicile (Wohnsitz, § 8) or a habitual abode (gewöhnlicher Aufenthalt, § 9) in Germany; a continuous presence of more than six months generally establishes a habitual abode. Such individuals are taxed on worldwide income.

legislation.title: UK Self Assessment — online filing deadline
legislation.summary: The deadline to file an online Self Assessment tax return and pay any tax due is 31 January following the end of the tax year (which runs 6 April–5 April).

legislation.title: Spain Renta (IRPF) — campaña anual
legislation.summary: The Spanish personal income tax (IRPF/Renta) campaign for a calendar year runs in the following spring and typically closes at the end of June.

legislation.title: Germany Einkommensteuererklärung — statutory deadline
legislation.summary: The German income tax return (Einkommensteuererklärung) for a year is generally due by 31 July of the following year; later dates apply where a tax adviser files.

legislation.title: Spain–UK Double Taxation Convention — Article 6 (rental)
legislation.summary: Income from immovable property (e.g. rental income) may be taxed in the country where the property is situated; the residence country relieves resulting double taxation. The applicable rate and relief are computed by the engine from this convention.

legislation.title: Spain–UK Double Taxation Convention — Article 10 (dividends)
legislation.summary: Dividends arising in one country and paid to a resident of the other may be taxed in the source country at a reduced rate set by this convention, with relief given in the residence country. The exact capped rate is computed by the engine.

legislation.title: Spain–UK Double Taxation Convention — Article 11 (interest)
legislation.summary: Interest arising in one country and paid to a resident of the other may be taxed in the source country at a reduced rate set by this convention, with relief given in the residence country. The exact capped rate is computed by the engine.

legislation.title: Spain–UK Double Taxation Convention — Article 14 (employment)
legislation.summary: Income from employment is generally taxable in the country where the work is physically performed, subject to the convention's conditions; the residence country relieves double taxation.

legislation.title: Spain–UK Double Taxation Convention — Article 17 (pensions)
legislation.summary: Pensions and similar remuneration are allocated between the two countries according to this convention; the residence country relieves any double taxation.

legislation.title: Spain IRPF — general progressive scale (simplified)
legislation.summary: Spain's personal income tax (IRPF) applies a progressive scale to general income above the personal minimum (allowance). The illustrative estimate uses the state general brackets from 19% to 47% and the personal minimum as an allowance; it excludes savings-income rates, regional scales, deductions and social security.

legislation.title: UK Income Tax — rates and bands (rest-of-UK)
legislation.summary: UK income tax applies a personal allowance then basic (20%), higher (40%) and additional (45%) rates. The illustrative estimate uses the rest-of-UK bands and excludes the personal-allowance taper above £100,000, Scottish rates, dividend/savings allowances and National Insurance.

legislation.title: Germany Einkommensteuer — progressive zones (coarse approximation)
legislation.summary: Germany's income tax (§32a EStG) is a continuous progressive formula above the Grundfreibetrag (basic allowance), rising to 42% and 45%. The illustrative estimate approximates this with coarse marginal bands and excludes the solidarity surcharge, church tax, joint assessment and deductions.

legislation.title: Spain — Beckham / impatriate regime (Art. 93 LIRPF, as amended by the Startups Law)
legislation.summary: Spain's special regime for inbound workers (the 'Beckham regime', Art. 93 LIRPF), as amended by the Startups Law (Ley 28/2022, in force 2023). It MAY BE RELEVANT to a person newly moving their tax residence to Spain who was not Spanish tax resident in the previous 5 tax years. Where it applies and is elected, the taxpayer is taxed at a flat 24% on employment income up to EUR 600,000 (47% on the excess) for the arrival year plus the following 5 tax years, and broadly on Spanish-source rather than worldwide income. The Startups Law widened access to qualifying remote workers ('digital nomads'), entrepreneurs and certain highly-qualified professionals. Informational regime evidence — not an eligibility determination or a tax computation; confirm eligibility and figures at the cited source and with a qualified professional.
legislation.regime_name: Special regime for inbound workers (Beckham regime)

figures.label: flat rate on employment income up to cap
income cap for the flat rate
rate on the excess above the cap

eligibility_criteria: Becomes Spanish tax resident as a result of the move
Was not Spanish tax resident in the previous 5 tax years
Move linked to a qualifying employment, board/director role, entrepreneurial activity or qualifying remote work (digital nomad)
exclusions: Professional sportspeople covered by their own special rules
Individuals who do not formally elect the regime within the statutory window

legislation.title: Spain — Startups Law (Ley 28/2022) entrepreneur / digital-nomad framework
legislation.summary: Spain's Startups Law (Ley 28/2022) promotes the emerging-companies ecosystem and created a dedicated international-teleworking ('digital nomad') visa and broadened the Beckham impatriate regime. It MAY BE RELEVANT to entrepreneurs, startup founders and qualifying remote workers relocating to Spain. Among other measures it reduced the prior-non-residence condition for the impatriate regime to 5 tax years and extended access to entrepreneurs and remote workers. Informational regime evidence — not an eligibility determination; confirm at the cited source and with a professional.
legislation.regime_name: Startups Law — entrepreneur and digital-nomad routes

figures.label: prior non-residence condition for the impatriate regime

eligibility_criteria: Entrepreneur, startup founder or qualifying remote worker relocating to Spain
Activity meets the innovative / international-teleworking conditions in the law

legislation.title: Spain — Large-family deduction (Ley 40/2003; IRPF deducción por familia numerosa)
legislation.summary: Spain's large-family ('familia numerosa', Ley 40/2003) IRPF deduction MAY BE RELEVANT to a family that holds a valid official large-family title and carries on an economic activity or receives a contributory/assistance benefit. The deduction is up to EUR 1,200 per year for a general-category large family and up to EUR 2,400 per year for the special category, increased by up to EUR 600 per year for each child beyond the category minimum; it can be drawn in advance (Modelo 143). Informational regime evidence — not an eligibility determination; confirm at the cited source and with a professional.
eligibility_criteria: Holds a valid official large-family (familia numerosa) title
Carries on an economic activity or receives a qualifying contributory/assistance benefit

legislation.title: Spain — Golden visa real-estate investment route (REPEALED)
legislation.summary: Spain's residency-by-investment 'golden visa' real-estate route (EUR 500,000 property investment, formerly Ley 14/2013) was REPEALED with effect from 3 April 2025 by Ley Orgánica 1/2025. New applicants can no longer obtain Spanish residency through that EUR 500,000 property route; permits granted before 3 April 2025 remain valid and renewable. This is a closed route — do NOT recommend it for a new move. Informational regime evidence — confirm status at the cited source and with a professional.
exclusions: Closed to new applicants from 3 April 2025
legislation.grandfathering: Permits granted before 3 April 2025 remain valid and may be renewed under the prior rules.

legislation.title: Spain — Solidarity Tax on Large Fortunes (Impuesto de Solidaridad, ITSGF)
legislation.summary: Spain's national Solidarity Tax on Large Fortunes (ITSGF) MAY BE RELEVANT to high-net-worth residents: it applies to individual net wealth above EUR 3,000,000 and operates alongside the regional Wealth Tax, with the regional Wealth Tax paid credited against it. Because it is a national tax, it can effectively override regional Wealth-Tax rebates (such as Madrid's or Andalucía's) for net wealth above the threshold. Informational regime evidence — not an eligibility determination; confirm at the cited source and with a professional.
legislation.regime_name: Temporary Solidarity Tax on Large Fortunes (ITSGF)
eligibility_criteria: Individual net wealth above EUR 3,000,000

legislation.title: Spain (Madrid) — 100% regional Wealth-Tax rebate (bonificación)
legislation.summary: The Comunidad de Madrid applies a 100% rebate (bonificación) on the regional Wealth Tax, so Madrid-resident individuals broadly pay no regional Wealth Tax. This MAY BE RELEVANT to a high-net-worth person considering residence in Madrid, but note the national Solidarity Tax on Large Fortunes (ITSGF) can still apply to net wealth above EUR 3,000,000. Regional rule — applies only to Madrid; do not apply it to other Spanish regions. Informational regime evidence — confirm at the cited source and with a professional.
eligibility_criteria: Tax resident in the Comunidad de Madrid
exclusions: Does not remove the national Solidarity Tax (ITSGF) above EUR 3,000,000

legislation.title: Spain (Andalucía) — 100% regional Wealth-Tax rebate (bonificación)
legislation.summary: Andalucía applies a 100% rebate (bonificación) on the regional Wealth Tax, so Andalucía-resident individuals broadly pay no regional Wealth Tax. This MAY BE RELEVANT to a high-net-worth person considering residence in Andalucía, but the national Solidarity Tax on Large Fortunes (ITSGF) can still apply to net wealth above EUR 3,000,000. Regional rule — applies only to Andalucía. Informational regime evidence — confirm at the cited source and with a professional.
eligibility_criteria: Tax resident in Andalucía
exclusions: Does not remove the national Solidarity Tax (ITSGF) above EUR 3,000,000

legislation.title: Spain (Cataluña) — regional IRPF scale and Wealth Tax
legislation.summary: Spanish personal income tax (IRPF) is split into a state portion and a regional portion, so the total rate depends on the autonomous community of residence. Cataluña applies one of the higher combined top marginal IRPF rates (around 50% on the top band, broadly above EUR 300,000) and levies its regional Wealth Tax (top marginal rate around 3.75%). This MAY BE RELEVANT to a person moving to Barcelona/Cataluña, whose regional rates differ from, e.g., Madrid. Regional rule — applies only to Cataluña. Informational regime evidence — confirm at the cited source and with a professional.
legislation.regime_name: Catalonia regional IRPF and Wealth Tax

figures.label: approximate combined top marginal IRPF rate

eligibility_criteria: Tax resident in Cataluña

legislation.title: Spain (País Vasco) — foral (provincial) tax system
legislation.summary: The Basque Country (País Vasco) operates a 'foral' tax system under the Economic Agreement (Concierto Económico): each of its provinces sets its own personal income tax and wealth-tax rules, distinct from the common Spanish (territorio común) regime. This MAY BE RELEVANT to a person moving to the Basque Country, whose income and wealth tax can differ materially from common-territory Spain. Regional/foral rule — applies only to the Basque provinces. Informational regime evidence — confirm at the cited source and with a professional.
eligibility_criteria: Tax resident in a Basque (foral) province

legislation.title: Spain (Navarra) — foral tax system
legislation.summary: Navarra operates its own 'foral' tax system under the Economic Convention (Convenio Económico), setting personal income tax and wealth-tax rules distinct from the common Spanish regime. This MAY BE RELEVANT to a person moving to Navarra, whose income and wealth tax can differ from common-territory Spain. Regional/foral rule — applies only to Navarra. Informational regime evidence — confirm at the cited source and with a professional.
eligibility_criteria: Tax resident in Navarra

legislation.title: Portugal — Non-Habitual Resident (NHR) regime (CLOSED to new entrants)
legislation.summary: Portugal's Non-Habitual Resident (NHR) regime CLOSED to new entrants from 2024. Existing NHR holders keep the benefit for the remainder of their 10-year period, but new movers generally cannot register for the original NHR; the successor route is the tax incentive for scientific research and innovation (IFICI / 'NHR 2.0'). Do NOT recommend the original NHR for a new move. Informational regime evidence — confirm status at the cited source and with a professional.
exclusions: Closed to new entrants from 2024 (see IFICI for the successor regime)
legislation.grandfathering: Individuals already registered as NHR keep the benefit for the remainder of their 10-year period; limited transitional access applied for some who relocated by 2024.

legislation.title: Portugal — IFICI tax incentive for scientific research and innovation ('NHR 2.0')
legislation.summary: Portugal's tax incentive for scientific research and innovation (IFICI, the 'NHR 2.0' successor to the closed NHR) MAY BE RELEVANT to qualifying new residents in eligible scientific research, innovation, higher-education or qualifying high-value roles. Where it applies it provides a flat 20% rate on qualifying Portuguese employment and self-employment income and exemptions on certain foreign-source income, for up to 10 years. Note that, unlike the old NHR, foreign PENSIONS are generally taxed at the normal progressive rates under IFICI. Informational regime evidence — not an eligibility determination; confirm at the cited source and with a professional.
legislation.regime_name: Tax incentive for scientific research and innovation (IFICI / NHR 2.0)

figures.label: flat rate on qualifying PT employment/self-employment income

eligibility_criteria: Becomes Portuguese tax resident and was not resident in the prior years required by the rules
Works in a qualifying scientific research, innovation, higher-education or eligible high-value role
exclusions: Foreign pensions are taxed at normal progressive rates (no pension exemption)

legislation.title: Italy — Impatriate workers regime (2024 reform)
legislation.summary: Italy's impatriate-workers regime (lavoratori impatriati), as reformed for arrivals from 2024, MAY BE RELEVANT to a qualifying worker moving their tax residence to Italy after a required period of non-residence. Where it applies, 50% of qualifying employment/self-employment income produced in Italy is exempt (so half is taxed), on income up to EUR 600,000 per year, for 5 years (with possible extension). The 2024 reform reduced the exemption from the previous 70%/90% levels and added the EUR 600,000 cap. Informational regime evidence — not an eligibility determination; confirm at the cited source and with a professional.
eligibility_criteria: Becomes Italian tax resident after the required period of prior non-residence
Performs qualifying employment or self-employment work mainly in Italy

legislation.title: Italy — HNWI flat tax on foreign income (neo-domiciled regime)
legislation.summary: Italy's flat-tax regime for new-resident high-net-worth individuals MAY BE RELEVANT to a wealthy person moving their tax residence to Italy after a required period of non-residence. It substitutes a fixed annual amount for tax on FOREIGN-source income: EUR 200,000 per year for new electors who became resident after 10 August 2024 (EUR 100,000 for those who elected earlier), plus EUR 25,000 per year for each included family member, for up to 15 years. Informational regime evidence — not an eligibility determination; confirm at the cited source and with a professional.
legislation.regime_name: Flat tax for new resident high-net-worth individuals

figures.label: annual flat tax on foreign income (new electors from 10 Aug 2024)
annual flat tax for earlier electors
additional annual amount per family member

eligibility_criteria: Moves tax residence to Italy after the required period of prior non-residence
Formally elects the substitute flat tax on foreign-source income

legislation.title: Italy — 7% flat tax for foreign-pension holders relocating to southern Italy
legislation.summary: Italy offers a 7% flat tax on all foreign-source income to individuals who hold a foreign pension and move their tax residence to a small town (generally under 20,000 inhabitants) in southern Italy, for up to 10 years. This MAY BE RELEVANT to a retiree with foreign pension income relocating to qualifying municipalities. Informational regime evidence — not an eligibility determination; confirm at the cited source and with a professional.
legislation.regime_name: 7% flat tax for foreign pensioners in southern Italy
eligibility_criteria: Receives a foreign-source pension
Moves tax residence to a qualifying small town in southern Italy
Was not Italian tax resident in the prior period required by the rules

legislation.title: Greece — 7% flat tax for foreign-pension holders (Art. 5B ITC)
legislation.summary: Greece's regime for foreign pensioners (Art. 5B of the Income Tax Code) MAY BE RELEVANT to a retiree with foreign-source pension income who transfers their tax residence to Greece after a required period of non-residence. Where it applies, all foreign-source income is taxed at a flat 7%, for up to 15 years. Informational regime evidence — not an eligibility determination; confirm at the cited source and with a professional.
eligibility_criteria: Receives foreign-source pension income
Transfers tax residence to Greece after the required prior non-residence
Moves from a country with an administrative-cooperation agreement with Greece
exclusions: Cannot be combined with the Art. 5A non-dom or Art. 5C impatriate regimes

legislation.title: Greece — 50% exemption for inbound employees/self-employed (Art. 5C ITC)
legislation.summary: Greece's impatriate regime (Art. 5C of the Income Tax Code) MAY BE RELEVANT to a person who transfers their tax residence to Greece to take up new employment or self-employment there. Where it applies, 50% of Greek employment or business income is exempt from income tax (and from the special solidarity contribution) for 7 years. Informational regime evidence — not an eligibility determination; confirm at the cited source and with a professional.
eligibility_criteria: Transfers tax residence to Greece after the required prior non-residence
Takes up new employment or self-employment in Greece
Commits to remain a Greek tax resident for the minimum required period
exclusions: Cannot be combined with the Art. 5A non-dom or Art. 5B pensioner regimes

legislation.title: Greece — Non-dom EUR 100,000 lump-sum regime (Art. 5A ITC)
legislation.summary: Greece's non-dom regime (Art. 5A of the Income Tax Code) MAY BE RELEVANT to a high-net-worth individual who transfers their tax residence to Greece and makes a qualifying investment of at least EUR 500,000. Where it applies, foreign-source income is covered by a fixed annual lump-sum tax of EUR 100,000 (plus EUR 20,000 per included family member), for up to 15 years. Informational regime evidence — not an eligibility determination; confirm at the cited source and with a professional.

figures.label: annual lump-sum tax on foreign income
additional annual amount per family member

eligibility_criteria: Transfers tax residence to Greece after the required prior non-residence
Makes a qualifying investment of at least EUR 500,000
exclusions: Cannot be combined with the Art. 5B pensioner or Art. 5C impatriate regimes

legislation.title: France — Impatriate regime (Art. 155 B CGI)
legislation.summary: France's impatriate regime (régime des impatriés, Art. 155 B CGI) MAY BE RELEVANT to an employee or manager recruited from abroad who becomes French tax resident and was not French resident in the previous 5 calendar years. Where it applies, the impatriation bonus is exempt and a 50% exemption can apply to certain foreign-source passive income (dividends, interest, royalties and gains), for up to 8 years. Informational regime evidence — not an eligibility determination; confirm at the cited source and with a professional.
legislation.regime_name: Impatriate regime (régime des impatriés, Art. 155 B CGI)

figures.label: exemption on qualifying foreign passive income

eligibility_criteria: Recruited from abroad to take up employment or a management role in France
Becomes French tax resident and was not French resident in the previous 5 calendar years
exclusions: Individuals who moved to France on their own initiative outside a qualifying assignment

legislation.title: Netherlands — Expat ruling (30% ruling, moving to a flat 27% from 2027)
legislation.summary: The Netherlands' expat ruling (the former '30% ruling') MAY BE RELEVANT to a qualifying employee recruited from abroad with scarce specific expertise. It allows a tax-free reimbursement of a percentage of salary: 30% for 2025 and 2026, then a flat 27% from 1 January 2027 for new users (the earlier 30/20/10 step-down was cancelled). It is capped at the public-sector ('WNT') salary norm and requires a minimum salary level; pre-2024 users keep their prior terms transitionally. Informational regime evidence — not an eligibility determination; confirm at the cited source and with a professional.
eligibility_criteria: Employee recruited from abroad (resided beyond the qualifying distance before hiring)
Has scarce specific expertise and meets the minimum-salary norm
exclusions: Reimbursement is capped at the public-sector (WNT) salary norm

legislation.title: United Kingdom — Non-dom remittance basis (ABOLISHED 6 April 2025)
legislation.summary: The UK's domicile-based remittance basis for non-domiciled individuals was ABOLISHED from 6 April 2025 and replaced by a residence-based system, the centrepiece of which is the 4-year Foreign Income and Gains (FIG) regime. New movers can no longer claim the old remittance basis; do NOT recommend it for a new arrival. Transitional reliefs (such as the Temporary Repatriation Facility) apply to former users. Informational regime evidence — confirm status at the cited source and with a professional.
exclusions: Abolished from 6 April 2025 — see the 4-year FIG regime for the successor

legislation.title: United Kingdom — 4-year Foreign Income and Gains (FIG) regime
legislation.summary: The UK's 4-year Foreign Income and Gains (FIG) regime (in force from 6 April 2025) MAY BE RELEVANT to a person becoming UK tax resident after at least 10 consecutive tax years of non-UK residence. Where it applies, qualifying foreign income and gains can be exempt from UK tax for the first 4 tax years of residence, but claimants forfeit the personal allowance and the capital-gains annual exempt amount for those years. Informational regime evidence — not an eligibility determination; confirm at the cited source and with a professional.

legislation.title: Andorra IRPF — general progressive scale (simplified)
legislation.summary: Andorra's personal income tax (IRPF) applies a simplified progressive scale: a personal minimum exempts the first EUR 24,000, the next slice up to EUR 40,000 is taxed at 5% and income above EUR 40,000 at 10%. The illustrative estimate excludes family/dependant reductions, savings-income rules and other reliefs.
